_meta.notes: Rust-Hand-faction ascended form (default). The Rust Hand traffics in iron's slow inevitability. The Ascendant has stopped fighting the rust and started conducting it.

tagline: You stopped fighting the rust. You started conducting it.

negativeVocab.rule: Rust Hand Ascendants do not gleam. Vocabulary of polish does not fit.

hardMoves.rule: Partial-success: pick one. The Ascendant's costs land in iron going wrong slowly.

moves.narrative: A join you forged today will fail in a year. You can already see it.
The iron took the rust at the wrong angle. You let it.
A tool that has served you many years will not see another season.
A rust-mark on your own forearm has spread.
An iron blank sings under your hammer in a frequency you did not strike. The Long Wyrm has hummed under your forge.

sampleCorpus.rule: Rust-Hand prose is unhurried, attentive to slow change, comfortable with imperfection.

passages.scenario: The Rust Hand Ascendant inspects a forge piece a year after forging it
passages.text:
The piece has come back. The customer who took it home a year ago has brought it for the first inspection.

You turn it in your hand. The mark you laid on the head has settled correctly; the rust has begun where you wanted it to begin and not where you didn't. You give the customer a single nod. They are pleased; they will be more pleased in another year, when the mark will deepen into the patina you predicted.

The rust is doing what you asked it to do. This took a long time to learn. Now it is yours.
